signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.37 — Trump's pause on 'Project Freedom' and Hormuz tensions drive a -0.95% intraday JPY bid as safe-haven demand competes with the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded carry trade vulnerable to further unwind if VIX exceeds 25. Intervention caution persists near the 160.00 handle despite the recent retreat.

signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Bias
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5952 — NZD leads G8 performance (+0.603%) while USD lags (-0.266%), creating a 0.869% spread. Despite a 150bp carry deficit (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%), NZD is supported by a hawkish RBNZ hike bias. COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) indicates a significant spec-CB divergence; if RBNZ confirms the hike bias on 27 May, a short-squeeze targets 0.6100.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7251 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 87% short creates an extreme institutional-retail divergence. NAB's call for a June RBA hike to 4.60% reinforces the 60bp AUD carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%). Retail capitulation risk rises if price holds above 0.7300, potentially accelerating the institutional long trend.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Macro
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1737 — Correlation with EuroStoxx at +0.717 vs norm of -0.149 (z=+1.99σ) signals a regime shift where EUR strength is tracking equity inflows rather than traditional rate differentials. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% maintains a 175bp USD carry advantage, but the pause in 'Project Freedom' headlines provides a tailwind for EUR. Watch for a return to negative correlation to signal a resumption of carry-dominant pricing.
